hero.question: Will Emmanuel Grégoire win the Paris mayor election?
hero.prob_take:
Eighty-one percent says Grégoire is the heavy favorite, but he's been bleeding 3.5 points in 24 hours, which suggests either new uncertainty about his path to victory or smart money rotating out ahead of the April election.

The real question is whether this reflects genuine tightening in the race or just normal volatility in a market that's still three months out, because if Grégoire's Socialist machine is as dominant as the odds imply, selling at 81 into uncertainty looks like panic rather than signal.

daily_take.headline: Grégoire's Paris mayoralty just got cheaper. Smart money knows why.
daily_take.deck: Emmanuel Grégoire dropped 3.5 points overnight to 81%, signaling real doubt about France's left-wing consensus pick. The timing matters: this isn't noise. Bettors are either pricing in late-breaking opposition strength or recognizing that 81% leaves no room for error. At this price, you're paying peak confidence on a race that still has 6 months of campaign chaos ahead. The sharp play is fading the chalk until we see actual polling movement to justify holding it.

sidebar.headline: CDU's 15-point collapse in 48 hours screams panic betting, not fundamental weakness in German center-right dominance.
sidebar.label: CDU Rhineland-Palatinate: 62.5%

sidebar.headline: Vitality's momentum spike to 57.5% is real, but this is a single esports tournament. Don't chase the 9-point move.
sidebar.label: Vitality Blast Rotterdam: 57.5%

sidebar.headline: Anduril's 22-point crater to 18.5% looks like forced selling, not a signal the IPO timeline actually shifted. Oversold.
sidebar.label: Anduril IPO 2026: 18.5%
